content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: noun + **be** in the correct form + past participle (+ **by**/**with** + noun)

rows: present simple
*Scientists launch satellites into orbit every year.*

am/is/are + past participle
*Satellites **are launched** into orbit every year.*
present continuous
*Engineers are upgrading the old railway bridge.*

am/is/are + -ing + past participle
*The old railway bridge **is being upgraded**.*
present perfect simple
*Has anyone translated this ancient manuscript?*

has/have + been + past participle
***Has** this ancient manuscript **been translated**?*
past simple
*They performed the symphony in a grand concert hall.*

was/were + past participle
*The symphony **was performed** in a grand concert hall.*
past continuous
*We waited outside while they were setting up the stage.*

was/were + -ing + past participle
*We waited outside while the stage **was being set up**.*
past perfect simple
*An athlete had broken the world record by the time the Games ended.*

had + been + past participle
*The world record **had been broken** by the time the Games ended.*
will future
*The team will launch the new spacecraft in six months.*

will + be + past participle
*The new spacecraft **will be launched** in six months.*
be going to future
*Local campaigners are going to challenge the new motorway development.*

is/are going to + be + past participle
*The new motorway development **is going to be challenged** by local campaigners.*
future perfect simple
*Researchers will have completed all the archaeological excavations by the end of summer.*

will + have + been + past participle
*All the archaeological excavations **will have been completed** by the end of summer.*
modal
*You should clean the telescope lens carefully before using it.*

modal + be + past participle
*The telescope lens **should be cleaned** carefully before use.*
modal + perfect infinitive
*They should have published the research findings by now.*

modal + have + been + past participle
*The research findings **should have been published** by now.*
-ing (gerund)
*I don't like people interrupting me during rehearsals.*

being + past participle
*I don't like **being interrupted** during rehearsals.*

content.subtitle: Use
items: When we don't know who does/did something: *My bicycle **has been stolen**!*
When it's obvious who does/did something: *A teenager **was arrested** near the park yesterday for vandalism.*
When it's not important who does/did something: *The old theatre **is being knocked down**.*
When we want to emphasise new information or use a formal style: *The telephone **was brought** to the world by Alexander Graham Bell.*

items.note: We do not normally use verbs in the passive in the present perfect continuous, past perfect continuous, future continuous or future perfect continuous tenses. Instead, we use a different phrase.

items.correct: The bridge has been **under construction** for four years.

items.incorrect: The bridge has been being built for four years.

items.correct: The athlete has been **in training** for the marathon for three years.

items.incorrect: The athlete has been being trained for the marathon for three years.

items.note: We only normally use 'by' to say who did something when it is important information.

items.correct: The telephone was invented **by** Alexander Graham Bell.
The finest violins are made **by** Italian craftsmen.

items.incorrect: Conductor! This piece has been played incorrectly by someone.

items.note: We usually use 'with' when we talk about the thing used to do something.

items.correct: The wound should then be cleaned **with** a cloth.

items.incorrect: The wound should be cleaned by a cloth.

items.note: Some verbs are not normally used in the passive. They include intransitive verbs (without objects), such as *appear* and *die*, and some common transitive verbs, such as *have*, *let*, *lack*, etc.

content.description: To express other people's opinions in a formal style, we can use two special forms of the passive. They can be used with a number of verbs, including: *say, believe, think, claim, estimate,* etc. Some other verbs (*argue, suggest, calculate,* etc) are usually used with only the second structure.

content.subtitle: Form
items: noun + *is/are said to* + bare infinitive/perfect infinitive

rows: People **think** he **is** an outstanding astronomer.

He **is thought to be** an outstanding astronomer.
It **is thought that** he **is** an outstanding astronomer.
People **believe** he **was** a pioneering scientist.

He **is believed to have been** a pioneering scientist.
It **is believed that** he **was** a pioneering scientist.
People **claim** he **has had** an influence on many other researchers.

He **is claimed to have had** an influence on many other researchers.
It **is claimed that** he **has had** an influence on many other researchers.
People **say** he **has been conducting** groundbreaking research in the field for over thirty years.

He **is said to have been conducting** groundbreaking research in the field for over thirty years.
It **is said that** he **has been conducting** groundbreaking research in the field for over thirty years.
People **estimated** that his laboratory **was** worth over $10 million.

His laboratory **was estimated to be** / **to have been** worth over $10 million.
It **was estimated** that his laboratory **was** worth over $10 million.
People **have suggested** that he **is** the most important physicist of his generation.
It **has been suggested** that he **is** the most important physicist of his generation.

content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: noun + **have**/**get** in the correct form + noun + past participle (+ **by**/**with** + noun)

rows.label: To show that someone arranges for someone else to do something for them
items: I **have** my car **serviced** at the garage once a year.
We **are having** a new security system **installed** tomorrow.
We **had** a professional designer **hired** for the project.
We **are going to have** the stage set **built** by a specialist company.

rows.label: To refer to an unpleasant situation which hasn't been arranged
items: We **had** our car **broken into** while we were at the concert.
The Robinsons **have had** their new laptop **stolen**.

items.note: Using the verb *get* is usually more informal than using *have*.

items.correct: Can you go and **get** this document **photocopied** for me?

items.note: We can also use *get somebody to do* and *have somebody do* when we want to refer to the person we arrange to do something for us.

items.correct: Why don't you **get the technician to repair** your computer?
Why don't you **have the technician repair** your computer?

content.description:
Some verbs can be followed by both a direct and an indirect object (usually a person). These verbs include:
*bring, buy, get, give, lend, make, offer, owe, pass, promise, send, show, take, teach, tell, write,* etc.

rows:
We can put the indirect object either immediately after the verb, or at the end of the sentence with a preposition (*for*/*to*, etc.).
A colleague lent **my brother** this history book.
A colleague lent this history book **to my brother**.

The subject of the sentence can be either the indirect object or the direct object of the active sentence.
**My brother** was lent this history book by a colleague.
**This history book** was lent to my brother by a colleague.

sections.title: A: Passive forms
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: The ancient monument **was / has** damaged during the earthquake last century.
When I walked past the science lab, the new equipment **was / has being** installed.
Our sports coach **was / has** told us to arrive early for tomorrow's training session.
I'm certain my bicycle **was / has** been stolen from outside the station!
Elena **was / has chosen** to represent the country at the international athletics championship.
I'm working in the library because the main classroom **is being painted / has been** painting.
This photograph **was / has probably** taken during the expedition to the Arctic.
Your science projects must **be / have handed** in by Friday afternoon.
Someone **was / has** left their umbrella on the bus.
Did you hear about the art gallery **being / having** broken into?
The play *Hamlet* **was / has written** by William Shakespeare.
It was a real shock when the team captain **was / has fired** from the squad.
Explorers had sailed the seas for centuries before the New World **was / has discovered** by Columbus.
When we arrived at the concert venue, we found out that the performance **was / had** been cancelled.
**Was / Has your** entry fee for the science exhibition paid for by the school or your parents?

sections.title: B: Passive transformation
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct passive form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: The moon ...... (hold) in orbit by the Earth's gravitational pull.
The original score of *Swan Lake* ...... (create) by Tchaikovsky in 1876.
The trophy for best screenplay ...... (present) later this evening at the awards ceremony.
By the time you return from your voyage, the suspect ...... (arrest) and charged.
I'm not sure whether our essays ...... (mark) yet or not.
X-rays ...... (discover) by Wilhelm Röntgen in 1895.
You wouldn't believe it now, but the famous author ...... (bully) throughout his school years.
Your application ...... (consider) and we will contact you as soon as we have reached a decision.
The temperature in the chamber can ...... (lower) by adjusting the thermostat.
The ancient oak tree ...... (blow) over in last night's storm.
The violin ...... (play) in orchestras for around four centuries now.
Two suspects ...... (question) at this moment by detectives in connection with the art theft.

sections.title: C: Write sentences in the passive

items.sentence: The cathedral / restore / a team of architects / at the moment.
items.answer: The cathedral is being restored by a team of architects at the moment.

items.sentence: A window / break / a stone / during the protest last night.
items.answer: A window was broken with a stone during the protest last night.

items.sentence: The telephone / invent / Alexander Graham Bell.
items.answer: The telephone was invented by Alexander Graham Bell.

items.sentence: At the audition last week, she / hear / the director / and she / offer / a leading role in the production.
items.answer: At the audition last week, she was heard by the director and she was offered a leading role in the production.

items.sentence: I decided to apply because I / tell / it was an excellent programme / my professor.
items.answer: I decided to apply because I had been told it was an excellent programme by my professor.

items.sentence: This painting / create / a local artist.
items.answer: This painting was created by a local artist.

items.sentence: It seemed as though the lock / force / a tool / at some point.
items.answer: It seemed as though the lock had been forced with a tool at some point.

items.sentence: The patient / give / a vaccination / a new type of needle / the nurse.
items.answer: The patient was given a vaccination with a new type of needle by the nurse.

items.sentence: The fastest lap of the race / set / Hamilton / a remarkable overtaking move.
items.answer: The fastest lap of the race was set by Hamilton with a remarkable overtaking move.

items.sentence: Your results / send / last Thursday and / should / deliver / to you by the end of the week.
items.answer: Your results were sent last Thursday and should be delivered to you by the end of the week.

sections.title: D: Rewrite using the phrase given

items.sentence:
They have been building the new museum for a long time now. **under construction**
......
items.answer: The new museum has been under construction for a long time now.

items.sentence:
They had been training the swimmer for the championship for over a year. **in training**
......
items.answer: The swimmer had been in training for the championship for over a year.

items.sentence:
They have been discussing the proposal at the international conference. **under discussion**
......
items.answer: The proposal has been under discussion at the international conference.

items.sentence:
They had been observing the patient for the past two weeks. **under observation**
......
items.answer: The patient had been under observation for the past two weeks.

items.sentence:
They have been using this telescope for over 25 years now. **in use**
......
items.answer: This telescope has been in use for over 25 years now.

items.sentence:
They have been developing the new space probe for over ten years. **in development**
......
items.answer: The new space probe has been in development for over ten years.

sections.title: E: Impersonal passive
sections.instructions: Rewrite in the passive, starting with the words given.

items.sentence:
People say that Iceland is a land of spectacular natural beauty.
It is said ......
items.answer: that Iceland is a land of spectacular natural beauty.

items.sentence:
People generally think that artificial intelligence will transform the way we work.
It is generally thought ......
items.answer: that artificial intelligence will transform the way we work.

items.sentence:
It is generally said that modern architecture lacks the character of older buildings.
Modern architecture is generally said ......
items.answer: to lack the character of older buildings.

items.sentence:
People often argue that social media does more harm than good.
It is often argued ......
items.answer: that social media does more harm than good.

items.sentence:
People have suggested that the library should extend its opening hours.
It has been suggested ......
items.answer: that the library should extend its opening hours.

items.sentence:
People say that playing the violin requires years of dedicated practice.
Playing the violin is said ......
items.answer: to require years of dedicated practice.

items.sentence:
It is said that the Vikings reached North America before Columbus.
The Vikings are said ......
items.answer: to have reached North America before Columbus.

items.sentence:
People think that global warming is caused by burning fossil fuels.
Global warming is thought ......
items.answer: to be caused by burning fossil fuels.

items.sentence: We ...... a new alarm system installed this week.
Why don't you get an expert ...... at your roof?
My car was making a strange noise so I ...... by the mechanic.
Marcus is thinking of having .......
My brother ...... his appendix removed at the hospital last week.
We didn't want to carry the equipment ourselves, so we got it .......
You should ...... a specialist to inspect the wiring before you move in.
The gallery owner wanted to have a local artist ...... a mural on the entrance wall.
Can we ...... before the rainy season starts?
options: B get the roof to repair

items.sentence: They ...... while they were travelling abroad.

sections.title: G: Causative transformation
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
Someone has damaged the sculpture in the town square! **been**
The sculpture ...... in the town square!

Sarah is having an operation right now. **being**
Sarah ...... right now.

Has Fiona remembered to invite you to her party? **been**
Have ...... party?

Dickens probably wrote this novel in 1859. **written**
This novel ...... Dickens in 1859.

The council is considering a new plan to reduce traffic in the city centre. **considered**
A new plan to reduce traffic in the city centre ......

My mum asked me to cycle to Megagrocer's for some supplies. **sent**
I ...... my mum.

I didn't follow the instructions so I found my teacher and she explained them to me. **got**
I didn't follow the instructions so I ...... them to me.

sections.title: H: Error correction (extra words)
sections.instructions: Find the extra word in each line. Click it.

items.sentence: The theatre production had been being prepared for months.
I had the director [to] present me with a rehearsal schedule and had got the crew to submit their technical requirements.
I also wanted to have us a well-known composer write the incidental music, and I arranged that, too.
The costumes were been being tailored by a specialist team, and I had asked the designer to use real silk.
Finally, the opening night got arrived.
They called me and said the main backdrop had been being torn!
I had my stage manager quickly to source a replacement from a local supplier.
I had got my producer to negotiate with the insurance company to recover the costs.

sections.title: I: Rewrite with indirect object at the end
sections.instructions: Rewrite with the indirect object at the end of the sentence.

items.sentence: My uncle got me an exciting book about space exploration!
items.answer: My uncle got an exciting book about space exploration for me.

items.sentence: Maria sent her professor a very thoughtful email.
items.answer: Maria sent a very thoughtful email to her professor.

items.sentence: She passed Marcus the newspaper.
items.answer: She passed the newspaper to Marcus.

items.sentence: The museum guide showed us a fascinating artefact.
items.answer: The museum guide showed a fascinating artefact to us.

items.sentence: The sports centre provides its members with free parking.
items.answer: The sports centre provides free parking to its members./The sports centre provides free parking for its members.

items.sentence: Our music teacher taught us this classical piece.
items.answer: Our music teacher taught this classical piece to us.

sections.title: J: Cloze (Passive)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: It is difficult to imagine what (1) ...... shown in the very first public cinemas before the film projector (2) ...... developed (3) ...... the Lumière brothers in 1895.
Many of the techniques which today (4) ...... regarded as standard in filmmaking (5) ...... completely unknown to these early pioneers.
Sound, colour and special effects (6) ...... all introduced gradually over the following decades.
Until then, films had (7) ...... produced as short, silent images using basic projection equipment.
Of course, the principle of moving images (8) ...... been explored by inventors and scientists for many years before this.
When cinema (9) ...... first shown to a paying public in Paris, it caused tremendous excitement.
It (10) ...... considered an extraordinary scientific curiosity and people queued for hours to see it.
There is a famous story of Edison, an American inventor, who (11) ...... short films made in his own studio and (12) ...... his engineers to develop a commercial viewing device.
People (13) ...... captivated by the new technology, and within just a few years, cinemas had opened in cities across the world.
